title: Designing a Few-Shot Prompt
concept: Use high-quality exemplars, format them consistently, place the query at the end, and keep instructions clear to maximize accuracy.

exercises.prompt: Why do we include examples (shots) in a prompt?

options.text: To make the AI run faster
To show the AI examples of the desired output format and style
To test if the AI knows how to count

exercises.explain: Few-shot prompting is all about giving the AI a few examples (shots) to show it how to perform a task.

cards.title: Get Perfect Answers Every Time
cards.body: Have you ever asked an AI to do something, only for it to give you a messy, chaotic answer? There is a simple way to fix this: show, don't just tell! By building a few-shot prompt, you can train the AI with examples in seconds.

cards.title: What is Few-Shot Prompting?
cards.body: Few-shot prompting is a technique where you show an AI a few completed examples (exemplars) of a task before asking it to solve a new one. By seeing these patterns, the AI understands the exact format and style you want.

cards.title: The Puppy Training Trick
cards.body: Think of few-shot prompting like teaching a puppy to sit. Instead of just saying 'sit' (zero-shot), you show them a treat, push their back down, and reward them three times in a row. By repeating this exact pattern, the puppy learns exactly what to do when you say 'sit' the fourth time.

cards.title: A Good Prompt in Action
cards.body:
Here is a well-designed few-shot prompt for translating slang:

Instructions: Translate the slang to standard English.

Input: 'This hits different.'
Output: 'This is uniquely good.'

Input: 'No cap.'
Output: 'No lie.'

Input: 'Spill the tea.'
Output:

cards.title: Myth: You Need Millions of Examples
cards.body: Many people believe that to train an AI, you need thousands of data points and complex programming. In reality, just 2 to 5 high-quality examples written in plain text inside your prompt can drastically improve the AI's accuracy!

cards.title: Messy vs. Structured Layouts
cards.body: See how structure changes everything. The bad prompt is messy and confuses the AI, while the good prompt uses a clean, predictable template.

left.text: Input: cold -> hot. Next is wet. Another one is up -> down. What is the opposite of dry?

right.text: Input: cold | Output: hot\nInput: up | Output: down\nInput: dry | Output:

exercises.prompt: Where should you place the actual question or query you want the AI to answer?

options.text: At the very beginning, before the instructions
Hidden inside one of the examples
At the very end of the prompt
It doesn't matter where it goes

exercises.explain: The prompt should always end with the actual question or input you want the AI to solve right now.

exercises.prompt: True or False: If you put typos or incorrect answers in your examples, the AI is likely to output typos and incorrect answers too.
exercises.explain: High-quality, correct examples are crucial because the AI will mimic any mistakes or bad formatting present in your examples.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the rule for good prompts.
exercises.sentence: To help the AI learn the pattern, you must format all your examples consistently.
exercises.explain: Keeping your examples formatted the same way helps the AI recognize the pattern easily.

exercises.prompt: We want to convert lowercase words to uppercase with an exclamation mark. Which AI correctly followed the pattern of this few-shot prompt?
exercises.explain: AI B is the AI because it perfectly followed the capitalization and punctuation pattern established by the examples, while AI A and C ignored the format.

exercises.prompt: You are building a few-shot prompt to categorize customer feedback. What is the ultimate recipe for success?

options.text: Mix different formats and place the question at the start
Provide no examples and let the AI guess the rules
Give clear rules, use a uniform format, and put the question last
Use only negative examples without showing the correct answers

exercises.explain: To get the best results, you must clearly outline the rules, keep the examples in a uniform layout, and put the actual question at the absolute end so the AI knows what to answer.
